inputs.name: image
inputs.semantic_hint: RGB image resized to 448x640, loaded from paths parsed by generate_subset(). Shape inferred from config.yml IMAGE_SIZE [448, 640] with 3 channels (implicit from RGB/object-detection domain).

ground_truths.name: bounding_boxes
ground_truths.semantic_hint: Bounding boxes with class IDs for object detection. Each image has a variable-length list of bounding boxes (max 150), each bbox is a list of integers parsed from annotation lines (likely [x1,y1,x2,y2,class_id] format). 3 categories: car, truck, pedestrian.

proposed_mapping.name: unknown
proposed_mapping.notes: RGB image tensor of shape [448, 640, 3] fed into YOLOv7 model

proposed_mapping.name: unknown
proposed_mapping.notes: Padded bounding box array of shape [150, 5] (x1, y1, x2, y2, class_id) used as ground truth for YOLO detection loss

unknowns: Only 2 Python files exist in the repository; no training loop, model-building code, or loss function is present in source. The repo appears to be a pre-integration skeleton.
The module 'webinar.utils.gcs_utils' imported by preprocess.py is missing from the repository, so the full data pipeline cannot be traced.

Exact bounding box format (number of fields per bbox) is unknown without access to actual annotation files. The 5-element [x1,y1,x2,y2,class_id] shape is inferred from YOLO convention but not directly confirmed in code.
No model.predict(), model.fit(), or loss function calls exist in the codebase to confirm how inputs/GTs are consumed by the model.
Image preprocessing (normalization, channel ordering) details are not present in code. PIXEL_MEAN [0,0,0] in config suggests no mean subtraction, but actual preprocessing logic is missing.
The 'code-loader-helpers' dependency in requirements.txt suggests Tensorleap integration code may exist elsewhere or be planned, but no such code is present in this repo.
